skill.name: security-audit
skill.description: Comprehensive security auditing workflow covering web application testing, API security, penetration testing, vulnerability scanning, and security hardening.
skill.summary: A workflow bundle that orchestrates multiple security skills for conducting penetration tests, vulnerability assessments, and security hardening.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All static findings are false positives. The 48 external_command detections are markdown code blocks using backticks for prompt examples, not actual shell execution. The Metasploit reference is a skill name to invoke, not tool execution. The weak crypto alerts are triggered by the word 'vulnerability' in context. This is a documentation-only workflow bundle with no executable code.

low_findings.title: False Positive: External Command Detection
low_findings.description: Static scanner detected 48 instances of backtick usage as 'external commands'. These are markdown code block delimiters for example prompts, not actual Ruby/shell execution. No command injection risk exists.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Backticks in markdown are formatting delimiters, not code execution operators. All 48 occurrences are in code block sections showing example prompts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Metasploit Framework Reference
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'Metasploit framework' as a blocker. This is a reference to an external skill name (@metasploit-framework) to invoke, not actual Metasploit tool usage.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 132 shows 'metasploit-framework' as a skill to invoke in the workflow, similar to other skill references. No actual penetration tool execution occurs.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 'weak cryptographic algorithm' at lines 3 and 192. These are false positives triggered by the word 'vulnerability' in the description and 'Using Components with Known Vulnerabilities' from OWASP Top 10 checklist.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: No cryptographic code or algorithms are present. The alerts are triggered by keyword matching on 'vulnerability' which appears in standard security documentation.

content.value_statement: This workflow bundle orchestrates multiple security skills to conduct full penetration tests, vulnerability assessments, and security hardening across web applications and APIs.
actual_capabilities: Orchestrate reconnaissance using scanning tools and Shodan
Execute automated vulnerability scanning for OWASP Top 10
Perform web application testing for injection flaws and XSS
Conduct API security testing with fuzzing techniques
Execute penetration testing workflows with planning checklists
Generate security hardening recommendations and reports
limitations: This is a workflow orchestrator, not an executable scanner
Requires other security skills to be available in the marketplace
Does not actually run penetration tools - only provides guidance
Report generation is manual documentation assistance only

use_cases.title: Security Consultant Auditing Client Web App
use_cases.description: Use the full workflow to conduct a comprehensive security assessment following industry-standard methodologies.

use_cases.title: Developer Securing Own Application
use_cases.description: Use vulnerability scanning and hardening phases to find and fix security issues before production deployment.

use_cases.title: DevSecOps Team Running Automated Scans
use_cases.description: Integrate scanning phases into CI/CD pipelines for continuous security validation.

prompt_templates.title: Start Full Security Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @security-audit to run a comprehensive security audit on my web application. Start with reconnaissance and work through all phases.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Vulnerability Scan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @security-audit to perform Phase 2 vulnerability scanning on my application. Focus on automated scanner results.

prompt_templates.title: API Security Test
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @security-audit to conduct Phase 4 API security testing. Test authentication, rate limiting, and input validation.

prompt_templates.title: Generate Security Report
prompt_templates.prompt: Use @security-audit Phase 7 reporting to document all findings and create a remediation plan for the vulnerabilities found.

output_examples.input: Run a security audit on my REST API
output_examples.output:
I will conduct a comprehensive security audit of your REST API following our structured workflow.

**Phase 1: Reconnaissance**
- Identify API endpoints and technology stack
- Gather intelligence on exposed services

**Phase 2: Vulnerability Scanning**
- Run automated vulnerability scanners
- Perform static analysis
- Scan dependencies for known vulnerabilities

**Phase 4: API Security Testing**
- Enumerate all API endpoints
- Test authentication mechanisms
- Test for rate limiting bypasses
- Test input validation and error handling

Shall I proceed with any specific phase?

output_examples.input: Help me test for SQL injection vulnerabilities
output_examples.output:
I will guide you through SQL injection testing using the @sql-injection-testing skill.

**Testing Approach:**
1. Identify injection points in your application
2. Test for SQL injection using manual techniques
3. Use automated tools to enumerate vulnerabilities
4. Document proof of concept for each finding

Would you like me to proceed with testing specific endpoints?

best_practices: Always obtain written authorization before testing any target
Follow the workflow phases in order for comprehensive coverage
Document all findings with proof of concept evidence
Use the OWASP Top 10 checklist as minimum coverage baseline
anti_patterns: Running penetration tests without proper authorization
Skipping reconnaissance and jumping directly to exploitation
Ignoring findings from automated scanners without manual verification
Failing to document findings with reproducible steps

faq.question: Does this skill actually run security scanners?
faq.answer: No. This is a workflow bundle that provides guidance and orchestrates other security skills. It does not execute actual scanning tools.

faq.question: Do I need other skills installed?
faq.answer: Yes. This workflow references other skills like scanning-tools, vulnerability-scanner, and pentest-commands. These must be available for full functionality.

faq.question: Is this tool safe to use on any website?
faq.answer: No. Only use on systems you own or have explicit written authorization to test. Unauthorized penetration testing is illegal.

faq.question: Can this generate a final security report?
faq.answer: The workflow includes Phase 7 for reporting, which helps structure findings and remediation steps. Final report generation requires manual compilation.

faq.question: What OWASP categories are covered?
faq.answer: The workflow covers all OWASP Top 10 categories including injection, broken authentication, sensitive data exposure, XXE, broken access control, security misconfiguration, XSS, insecure deserialization, vulnerable components, and insufficient logging.

faq.question: Does this work with Claude Code and Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. This skill supports claude, codex, and claude-code tools as specified in the supported_tools field.
